skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: AI video generation skill that creates finished multi-shot videos (5-120s) from text, images, URLs, scripts, or audio. Auto-selects the best model per shot from Seedance 2, Kling 3.0, HappyHorse, and 10+ models. Includes AI music, lip sync, subtitles, and transitions. No prompt engineering or model selection required.
skill.summary: AI video generation agent that produces finished multi-shot videos from text descriptions using auto model selection.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate API client for the Pexo AI video generation service. It uses shell scripts to communicate with pexo.ai's REST API, requiring network access, API credentials, and local script execution. The static analyzer flagged a high number of findings (537) due to the combination of these risk factors, but most are expected patterns for a CLI API client. Key confirmed risks are: external API calls to a third-party service, credential access (PEXO_API_KEY), and local file operations. No evidence of malicious intent or obfuscation was found. The skill is safe to publish with MEDIUM risk level and appropriate user warnings.

high_findings.title: API Credential Access via Environment Variable
high_findings.description: The skill reads PEXO_API_KEY from ~/.pexo/config or environment variables and uses it in Authorization headers for all API calls. This is required for the skill to function but means the API key is accessible to the AI agent and any process that can read the config file or environment.

high_findings.title: External Network Communication to Third-Party API
high_findings.description: All shell scripts make HTTPS requests to pexo.ai API endpoints. The base URL is configurable via PEXO_BASE_URL. This is the core functionality of the skill but involves data leaving the user's environment.

medium_findings.title: Shell Script Execution via Command Substitution
medium_findings.description: The skill uses shell command substitution ($(...)) extensively in bash scripts to call the Pexo API. This is the intended operation of the skill but means the AI agent executes arbitrary shell commands. Arguments are validated in some cases (file existence, asset ID format) but not universally sanitized.

medium_findings.title: Hidden File Access for Configuration
medium_findings.description: The skill reads configuration from ~/.pexo/config, a hidden file in the user's home directory. This is standard CLI tool behavior but grants filesystem access to credential-containing files. The config file stores the PEXO_API_KEY in plaintext.

medium_findings.title: Temporary File and Directory Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill creates temporary files using mktemp and stores downloaded assets in ~/.pexo/tmp/. These operations are standard for CLI tools but do involve writing to the filesystem.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation and Scripts
low_findings.description: Multiple hardcoded URLs reference pexo.ai domains. These are legitimate service endpoints being documented, not malicious exfiltration targets. The URLs point to the service API, help guides, and project pages.

low_findings.title: Standard Device File Access in Scripts
low_findings.description: Some scripts redirect output to /dev/null, which is a standard shell scripting pattern for suppressing output. This is not a security concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution with Variable Interpolation
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts inject user-provided filenames and project IDs into curl command arguments via command substitution. While filenames are validated (file existence check), there is no comprehensive input sanitization against shell metacharacters in file paths.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos with Auto Model Selection
content.value_statement: Creating professional AI videos is complex - you need to choose the right model, write prompts, and manage the production pipeline. Pexo Agent handles all of this by automatically routing each shot to the best model and producing a finished multi-shot video with music, subtitles, and transitions from a simple description.
actual_capabilities: Generate finished videos from text descriptions, images, URLs, scripts, or audio input
Automatically select the best AI video model for each shot from 10+ available models
Produce multi-shot videos with AI music, lip sync, subtitles, and scene transitions
Upload and reference custom media files as visual or audio references
Create videos in multiple aspect ratios: 16:9 landscape, 9:16 portrait, and 1:1 square
Revise existing videos by sending new instructions to an existing project
limitations: Requires a Pexo API key and active internet connection to function
Video generation takes 8-20 minutes depending on length and shot complexity
Cannot directly access web URLs - user-provided links must be downloaded before uploading
Only supports specific media formats: images (jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic), videos (mp4, mov, avi), audio (mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac)

use_cases.title: Marketing Video Production
use_cases.description: Create product videos, promotional clips, and brand videos from a product photo or URL. The AI handles scriptwriting, shot composition, music selection, and final assembly.

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts from a simple text description. The skill auto-selects the correct aspect ratio and model for short-form video.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: E-commerce Video at Scale
use_cases.description: Produce multiple product video variants for A/B testing from product catalogs. Upload product images and let the agent create consistent branded video content.
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce managers and online store owners

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 15-second promotional video about [your product or topic].
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple video from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Video with Reference Image
prompt_templates.prompt: [Upload a product photo] Create a product video for this item. The video should be 15 seconds long and focus on the product features.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a product image and wants a video ad

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Shot Brand Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second brand video with 3 shots. The first shot shows the product packaging, the second shows the product in use, and the third shows the product with a call to action. Use upbeat music and add subtitles.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user creating a structured multi-shot video

prompt_templates.title: Video Revision with Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the background music to something more energetic. Make the text captions larger and position them at the bottom center. Keep the same visuals.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to revise an existing video project

output_examples.input: Make a 15-second vertical video for a coffee shop that shows pouring a latte, the shop interior, and a customer smiling. Use warm colors and soft background music.
output_examples.output: A finished 15-second vertical (9:16) video with 3 shots: latte art being poured, cozy shop interior with warm lighting, and a customer enjoying coffee. Background music is soft acoustic. Professional transitions between shots. Subtitles appear at the bottom.

output_examples.input: Create a product showcase video using this photo of handmade candles [uploaded image].
output_examples.output: A 15-second product video featuring the uploaded candle image with slow zoom effects. The video includes text overlay showing the product name, soft ambient music, and a call-to-action at the end. The aspect ratio is 1:1 square format.

best_practices: Upload reference images or audio directly instead of providing web URLs - the skill cannot crawl web links
Describe your desired video output clearly including length, style, and target platform for best results
Check your Pexo credit balance before starting large projects to avoid mid-production interruptions
anti_patterns: Adding unnecessary creative details that override Pexo's professional video judgment - this produces lower quality videos
Calling pexo-chat.sh during the WAIT polling phase - this triggers duplicate video production
Truncating download URLs by removing query parameters - this causes 403 Forbidden errors on video links

faq.question: What is Pexo Agent?
faq.answer: Pexo Agent is an AI video generation skill for Claude Code. It acts as a bridge between you and the Pexo video generation API. You describe the video you want, and it handles all the technical steps automatically.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Pexo API key. Sign up at pexo.ai, create an API key from your account settings, and save it to ~/.pexo/config. New users receive bonus credits upon registration.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: A typical 15-second, 3-shot video renders in about 8 minutes. A 60-second brand video takes approximately 20 minutes. The skill polls the server every 60 seconds and notifies you when the video is ready.

faq.question: What video formats and aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Videos can be 5 to 120 seconds long. Supported aspect ratios are 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (portrait for TikTok and Reels), and 1:1 (square). The final output is an MP4 video file.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it has been generated?
faq.answer: Yes, you can send new instructions to the same project ID. The skill will submit your feedback to Pexo, which will generate a revised version of the video.

faq.question: How do I check my credit balance?
faq.answer: Run the pexo-entitlements.sh script to see your available credits and plan details. The skill also automatically checks your credit balance when a 429 (credit exhausted) error occurs.
